food_subgroup: Kopitiam set meal — soft white bread buns with kaya or butter, served with hot or cold beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a Singaporean kopitiam mini bun set. FOODON_00002473 (bread roll food product) is used as the closest parent for the bun component. The beverage component is not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A000N (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent for the bun component of this set meal. No FoodEx2 code exists for a combined bun-and-beverage kopitiam set.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps to a Singaporean kopitiam mini bun set meal.

preparation_method: steamed or baked soft white bread buns
spread with kaya (coconut-egg jam) and/or butter
served with hot or cold beverages such as kopi or teh

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a set comprising 2 soft white bread buns (approximately 60 g each, spread with kaya and/or butter) and 2 hot or cold beverages such as kopi or teh (approximately 130 ml each), totalling approximately 380 g.

nutrient_source.title: Kaya Toast with Butter — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA SR Legacy White Bread Roll and Kopi (Coffee with Condensed Milk) composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry exists for a 'Mini Bun Set' as a composite meal. Values are estimated by combining: (1) HPB Foodo entry for kaya toast / soft bun with kaya and butter (per 100 g of bun component), and (2) HPB Foodo / USDA SR Legacy data for kopi-o or teh-c (coffee or tea with condensed milk, per 100 g of beverage component). The set is modelled as 120 g bun component + 260 g beverage component = 380 g total. Per-100 g values are weighted composites. This is an estimation; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Singaporean kopitiam mini bun set. The GI of 70 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread (GI ~70, glucose reference). The bun component is soft white bread, which is the dominant glycemic contributor. The beverage component (kopi/teh with condensed milk and sugar) contributes additional sugar but does not substantially alter the composite GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 106.4 g carbohydrate in 380 g serving) / 100 ≈ 59.4. Carbohydrate per serving = 28.0 g per 100 g × 3.8 (serving factor) = 106.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and added sugar from condensed milk and kaya
hypertension.guidance: The Mini Bun Set contributes approximately 551 mg sodium per serving (145 mg/100 g × 380 g); individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one set per occasion and avoid adding extra sugar or condensed milk to beverages.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 70) and high glycemic load per serving (~59) driven by refined white bread and added sugars in kaya and beverages
type2_diabetes.guidance: The Mini Bun Set has a high GI of 70 and a glycemic load of approximately 59 per set, making it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion should be restricted to one bun with a sugar-free beverage and paired with a protein source to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter and condensed milk, and added sugar from kaya and sweetened beverages
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of approximately 10.6 g per serving and the high added sugar load from kaya and condensed milk in beverages may adversely affect LDL cholesterol and triglyceride levels; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this set to occasional consumption and request reduced-sugar beverage options.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mini Bun Set is a popular Singaporean kopitiam (traditional coffee shop) meal offered by chains such as Toast Box. A standard set comprises two soft, pillowy white bread buns — typically steamed or lightly toasted — spread generously with kaya (a sweet coconut-egg jam flavoured with pandan) and/or salted butter, accompanied by two hot or cold beverages, most commonly kopi (traditional Singaporean coffee brewed with a sock filter and served with condensed milk or evaporated milk) or teh (tea with condensed milk). The buns have a tender, slightly chewy crumb with a mildly sweet, buttery flavour profile; the kaya spread adds a fragrant, custard-like sweetness. Nutritionally, the set is energy-dense, providing approximately 665 kcal per set, with a high carbohydrate load from refined white bread and added sugars in kaya and sweetened beverages. The glycemic index is high (~70), making the set a significant postprandial glucose challenge. Sodium is moderate at approximately 551 mg per set. The set contains gluten, eggs, dairy, and coconut. It is generally considered halal and vegetarian but not vegan. Clinically, the set is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high glycemic load, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat and added sugar content. It is a culturally significant breakfast and snack item in Singapore, deeply embedded in kopitiam dining culture.
common_user_queries: What is a Mini Bun Set?
How many calories in a Toast Box Mini Bun Set?
Is the Mini Bun Set suitable for diabetics?
Is the Mini Bun Set halal?
What drinks come with the Mini Bun Set?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a 'Mini Bun Set' as a composite meal. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing HPB Foodo data for kaya toast / soft bun and HPB / USDA SR Legacy data for kopi/teh beverages. The GI is analogously estimated from white bread GI data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates for a set of 2 soft white bread buns with kaya/butter (120 g bun component) and 2 kopitiam beverages (260 g beverage component), totalling 380 g per serving. Per-100 g values are weighted composites. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source data for these component foods. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source data for these component foods. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source data for these component foods. 5. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite set. 6. The beverage component is assumed to be standard kopi or teh with condensed milk; sugar-free or kopi-o variants would substantially reduce sugar_g, energy_kcal, and glycemic load. 7. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g per 100 g reflects trace amounts from butter; this is within normal range for dairy-derived trans fats.
